docs.title: FLEET Mira documentation
docs.text:
FLEET Mira brings device operations, compliant tachograph archiving, driver-card and vehicle-unit analysis, fleet records, driving-licence checks, deadlines, finance documents and verified cloud backup into one Windows application.

Figure: FLEET Mira start dashboard with anonymised demonstration data.

docs.title: Choose your path
docs.text: Audience Start here Contents End users User manual Daily workflows, lists, records, analyses, documents and troubleshooting Administrators Administrator and integrator guide Deployment, users, services, security, audit, logs and maintenance API integrators DriverCard REST and Accounting REST Endpoints, payloads, idempotency and error handling Finance integrators Accounting CSV/ZIP Archive layout and field-level contracts Operations teams Cloud backup and restore Google Drive/WebDAV, database snapshots and disaster recovery

docs.title: Key principles
docs.text:
• Original C and M files remain byte-for-byte traceable through hashes and the immutable local archive.
  • Signature results are stored per signed section; invalid or unverifiable files are quarantined without being deleted.
  • Business changes and automatic imports are recorded in the global audit log.
  • All tachograph calculations use UTC. Region settings change formatting only.
  • Cloud backup is one-way from the local primary archive to one configured target.
  • Financial originals are cloud-verified before a foreign invoice can be verified or booked.
  • UI layout, language and region are stored per user.

docs.title: Product and legacy names
docs.text: FLEET Mira is the visible product name. Technical identifiers such as DLTNGDownloadTool.spec, database tables, API paths, existing configuration keys and the backup prefix DLTNG/<installation-id>/... remain unchanged for compatibility. They must not be renamed in an existing installation.

docs.title: Documentation status
docs.text: The documentation follows the current application menus and implemented services. Screenshots use a reproducible, isolated demonstration profile; they contain no production DDD files, credentials, URLs or personal records.

docs.title: 1. Deployment model
docs.text:
FLEET Mira is a Windows desktop application with background schedulers. The application directory contains Python modules or the PyInstaller build, static assets and translations. Mutable data is referenced by config.json and normally includes:

Item Default/role SQLite database state/archive.db; schema and business metadata Tachograph archive compressed .ddd.gz objects below the configured archive root Logs logs/app.log, REST logs and native_crash.log Configuration config.json; paths, sources, tasks and service defaults UI profile SQLite per-user JSON plus machine-local QSettings geometry

Use absolute service paths in production and grant write access only to the application service/user identity. Antivirus exclusions should be narrowly limited to proven performance bottlenecks and must never disable scanning of downloaded originals.

docs.title: Initial deployment
docs.text:
1. Deploy the build and the supplied empty deployment database.
  2. Review archive, database and log paths before importing data.
  3. Start FLEET Mira and sign in with admin / admin.
  4. Change the password to at least 12 characters.
  5. Create named users and test role restrictions.
  6. Configure sources/tasks, SMTP and backup in that order.
  7. Execute and document backup/restore and terminal-retention tests.

docs.title: 2. Users, roles and permissions
docs.text:
Passwords use Argon2id. Five failed attempts lock an account for 15 minutes. The last active administrator cannot be deactivated.

Figure: User, role, language and account administration.

Role Typical scope ADMIN complete administration, finance booking, restore and retention SUPPORT device/source/task operations, archive troubleshooting, read-only fleet/finance OPERATOR operational imports, fleet maintenance, licence checks, finance workflows AUDITOR read/report/export without business changes

The UI disables unauthorised actions, while repositories/services call PermissionService.require(...) again. Do not treat hidden/disabled controls as the security boundary.

Local administrator recovery is available with --reset-admin-password. It requires Windows administrator rights, resets only the initial admin credential to admin, unlocks the account and forces a new password. The password itself is never audited.

docs.title: 3. Languages, regions and UTC
docs.text:
New deployments default to English and en-GB. User profiles may select any registered language/region. Date pickers, numbers and currency follow the region. Database/API dates remain ISO. All tachograph timestamps, comparisons, day/week boundaries and compliance calculations currently remain UTC; locale must not introduce a time-zone conversion.

Translation catalogues live under translations/<language>.json. English is the fallback catalogue. Missing production keys log I18N_MISSING and fail the catalogue parity test.

Figure: Administrative application settings.

docs.title: 4. SQLite and migrations
docs.text:
Migrations run once at startup before repositories serve UI jobs. Use SQLite WAL/default locking as configured by the application; never copy a live database file for backup.

Operational recommendations:

  • keep database and archive on reliable local storage;
  • monitor free space and failed backup jobs;
  • use the built-in SQLite backup API snapshot;
  • test PRAGMA integrity_check after restore and during maintenance;
  • stop application and services before manual file-level maintenance;
  • never edit audit, signature or finance tables directly.

The deployment database must contain schema/default rows but no operational persons, vehicles, DDD files, documents, credentials or audit history.

docs.title: 5. Scheduler, sources and tasks
docs.text:
Background scheduling starts independently of an authenticated UI session and runs as SYSTEM. Supported source types are FTP and DLT API. Every network operation must have connect/read timeouts.

Task processing sequence:

flowchart TD
    A[Schedule or Run now] --> B[List matching remote objects]
    B --> C[Download to controlled local path]
    C --> D[Hash, classify and archive]
    D --> E[Parse/signature verify or RFID CSV import]
    E --> F[Create/update business links and deadlines]
    F --> G[Queue cloud backup]
    G --> H{Strict backup mode?}
    H -->|No| I[Mark processed / optional terminal deletion]
    H -->|Yes| J[Wait for verified cloud object]
    J --> I

A technical failure before durable archiving must not mark or delete the terminal object. Duplicate hashes resume pending backup/deadline work without creating duplicate archives.

docs.title: 6. Tachograph archive and signature trust
docs.text:
The content dispatcher classifies DRIVER_CARD, VEHICLE_UNIT or UNKNOWN. C files use G1 RSA/SHA-1 or G2 ECDSA chains; M files verify signed download sections for G1/G2/G2V2. cryptography is a required runtime dependency.

Signature result priority is INVALID → UNVERIFIABLE → VALID. Parser status maps to verified, verified-with-warnings or quarantine. Quarantine never deletes the original but blocks trusted linking, C/M comparison, compliance snapshots and “latest trusted” selection.

Trust-store updates are administrative, explicit and audited. Validate package source, manifest and pinned fingerprints. Do not silently fetch or trust certificates from an arbitrary server.

docs.title: 7. Audit and diagnostics
docs.text:
The global audit normalises device, tachograph, fleet, finance, user and backup events. Each entry should include UTC time, actor/user ID, role, origin, action, object type/ID, result, correlation ID and sanitised before/after data. Passwords, tokens, API keys and binary originals are prohibited.

app.log is the complete application log. It includes scheduler, downloads, imports, jobs, cache timings, backup and service errors. Technical messages are always English. native_crash.log is written by faulthandler for process-level failures after diagnostics initialisation; failures before Python startup cannot be captured there.

Figure: Audit evidence used together with technical diagnostics.

The UI heartbeat logs UI_STALL if the main event loop is blocked for more than one second. A recovery entry records the duration. Use job/request/correlation IDs to join UI, service and audit events.

docs.title: 8. UI job operation
docs.text:
Expensive reads/calculations use worker-local SQLite connections. Qt widgets are changed only in the GUI thread. Read jobs, serialised writes and low-priority prefetch use separate lanes. New filter/date requests cancel stale generations; tab closure cancels owned work.

Performance incidents should be investigated by separating queue, database, compute, render and total time from the job log. Do not reintroduce processEvents() loops or modal busy dialogs for long work.

docs.title: 9. Driving-licence checks and notifications
docs.text:
PC/SC reads UID with FF CA 00 00 00, normalises to uppercase hexadecimal and assigns at most one active UID per person. A UID cannot be active for multiple persons.

Terminal CSV fields are UID;DD.MM.YYYY;HH:mm:ss;hostname. File- and row-level fingerprints provide idempotency. Unknown/inactive/time-incompatible UIDs remain clarification records.

SMTP supports STARTTLS or TLS. Passwords are DPAPI-protected. Default rules produce driver reminders and a daily fleet-responsible report. A desktop/tray process must be running for scheduled sends; missed runs are caught up only on the same day. Outbox idempotency prevents duplicate automatic mail per rule/object/recipient/day.

Figure: SMTP recipients, rules and send-log administration.

docs.title: 10. Finance and document vault
docs.text:
Financial data is stored in cents and EUR. Foreign invoices require exact header/position totals and at least one cloud-verified original before verification. Booking makes business records immutable; corrections use cancellations or credits.

Original documents are never SQLite BLOBs. The financial vault stages, hashes, uploads, reads back and verifies each object. Metadata stores target, remote path/ID, hash, size, version and retention. Target deletion is blocked while documents reference it.

Accounting delivery is available through REST and CSV/ZIP. Only booked records are exportable. REST uses a persistent idempotent outbox.

docs.title: 11. Backup and disaster recovery
docs.text:
Exactly one Google Drive or HTTPS WebDAV target may be active. Client-side AES-256-GCM is optional and preselected. Activation requires exporting and confirming a recovery key.

Figure: Cloud target, backup queue and restore administration.

Archives are backed up immediately; database snapshots default to 02:00 UTC. Retention keeps 30 daily and 12 monthly database states. DDD cloud objects are never automatically removed. Details and runbooks are in Cloud backup and restore.

At least quarterly, restore a database snapshot and representative C/M objects to an isolated test directory, verify hashes/integrity and document the result.

docs.title: 12. REST and integrations
docs.text:
• DriverCard REST binds to 127.0.0.1 by default and exposes parsed card evaluations.
  • Accounting REST sends booked invoice metadata and originals to a configured HTTPS endpoint.
  • Mobile licence-check endpoints remain a separate fleet API surface when enabled.

Do not expose a local development endpoint directly to the Internet. Place authentication, TLS, rate limiting and network access controls in front of any externally reachable API.

docs.title: 13. Legacy technical identifiers
docs.text:
The visible product is FLEET Mira. The following are intentionally retained for compatibility:

  • repository/project folder and PyInstaller file DLTNGDownloadTool.spec;
  • technical executable/internal names where changing them would break upgrades;
  • SQLite table/column names, configuration keys and event codes;
  • DriverCard/API paths and payload fields;
  • remote root DLTNG/<installation-id>/..., manifest magic values and existing User-Agent identifiers.

These are protocol/storage identifiers, not user-facing product branding. Renaming them requires a separately versioned migration.

docs.title: 14. Security checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] named users only; initial admin password changed;
  • [ ] role matrix tested with a non-admin account;
  • [ ] database/archive ACLs restricted;
  • [ ] source, SMTP, backup and REST secrets protected;
  • [ ] WebDAV HTTPS certificate and Google OAuth client validated;
  • [ ] recovery key stored offline and access logged;
  • [ ] strict terminal deletion selected where policy requires it;
  • [ ] audit/log retention and log rotation monitored;
  • [ ] signature trust store current and controlled;
  • [ ] daily database backup and archive backlog monitored;
  • [ ] restore test completed and documented;
  • [ ] operator procedural documentation covers retention, scan and legal review.

docs.title: 15. Incident and recovery checklist
docs.text:
1. Stop automatic writes if integrity, ransomware or credential compromise is suspected.
  2. Preserve app.log, native_crash.log, configuration and relevant audit exports.
  3. Revoke/rotate affected source, SMTP, Google/WebDAV and API credentials.
  4. Verify local archive hashes and remote object manifests.
  5. Restore into an isolated path first; never overwrite the only remaining database copy.
  6. Run integrity/hash checks and reconcile backup states.
  7. Resume services only after root cause, credentials and recovery result are documented.

docs.title: 16. Build and release checks
docs.text: Before release, run application tests, translation parity, documentation verification, mkdocs build --strict --clean, PyInstaller smoke tests and a clean deployment-database start. Verify both German and English screenshots contain FLEET Mira branding and no operational data.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: FLEET Mira supports transport operators in collecting, archiving and evaluating digital tachograph data while connecting that data to operational fleet records. It is designed as a desktop application with background scheduling and a local SQLite database.

docs.title: Main navigation
docs.text: Area Purpose Dashboard Consolidated operational status and direct links to filtered workspaces Device Management FLEET Mira terminals, status, remote files and device diagnostics Tachodata Archive C/M import, verification, analysis, export, deletion and backup status Fleet Management people, vehicles, deadlines, licence checks, tachodata coverage and finance Administration sources, tasks, audit, notifications, backup, users, roles, logs and settings

docs.title: Data flows
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[FTP, DLT API or manual import] --> B[Hash and immutable local archive]
    B --> C{Content classification}
    C -->|C file| D[Driver-card parser and signature verification]
    C -->|M file| E[Vehicle-unit parser and signature verification]
    D --> F[Person, activities, deadlines and compliance]
    E --> G[Vehicle, activities, calibration and positions]
    B --> H[Verified cloud backup]
    I[(SQLite metadata and audit)] --> H

docs.title: Security and evidence
docs.text:
• Local passwords use Argon2id; accounts lock after repeated failed attempts.
  • Fixed roles are ADMIN, SUPPORT, OPERATOR and AUDITOR; services re-check permissions independently of the UI.
  • Secrets are DPAPI-protected on Windows and excluded from logs and audit.
  • Archived DDD files and cloud objects are hash-verified.
  • Logical deletion requires a reason. Audit entries cannot be edited through the application.
  • Database backups are produced through the SQLite backup API and pass integrity_check before upload.

docs.title: Supported languages and regions
docs.text: The application provides German, English and 21 additional European UI languages. Language and region are independent profile settings. English (en-GB) is the default for new deployments. Tachograph timestamps remain UTC regardless of region.

docs.title: Scope boundaries
docs.text: FLEET Mira provides technical controls and technical pre-checks. It does not replace legal assessment, employment-law decisions, national exception handling, a certified accounting system or the operator's procedural documentation.

docs.title: Detailed business guides
docs.text:
• Personnel and fleet tachodata evaluations
  • Driver-card analysis – every tab
  • Vehicle-unit analysis – every tab
  • Foreign invoices – workflow and every tab
  • Position maps for C and M data

These guides use a temporary anonymised copy of the development database. Activities, timestamps, amounts and approved coordinates remain representative; person, card and vehicle identities were replaced deterministically.

docs.title: 1. Sign-in and workspace
docs.text:
Start FLEET Mira from the installed shortcut. The splash screen is followed by the sign-in dialog. A new installation uses admin / admin once and forces a secure replacement password.

Figure: FLEET Mira splash screen before sign-in.

Figure: Local user sign-in.

After every sign-in, the Dashboard is the active page. The top navigation switches between the five main areas. Long operations show a non-modal loading status and may be cancelled; other tabs remain usable.

docs.title: User profile
docs.text:
Open User → My profile to change display name, email, language and region. Language or region changes take effect after the next sign-in. Table layouts, filters, splitter positions and column order are stored per user.

Figure: User-specific language, region and profile settings.

docs.title: Tables and context menus
docs.text:
• Click a header to sort; click again to reverse the direction.
  • Drag headers to change the column order and drag header boundaries to change widths.
  • Right-click a header to show or hide columns where offered.
  • Right-click a data row for object-specific actions. The row under the pointer is selected first.
  • Selected rows use a dark-blue background and white text, overriding status colours for readability.

docs.title: 2. Dashboard
docs.text:
The six photo-backed cards provide direct navigation:

Figure: Dashboard with anonymised demonstration data and direct navigation figures.

  • FLEET Mira devices: total, online and offline devices.
  • People/Vehicles: active master records.
  • Tachograph files: total C/M files, backup backlog/errors and latest imports.
  • Personnel deadlines / Vehicle deadlines: upcoming, due today and overdue items.
  • Driving-licence checks: pending clarification, checks due soon, overdue checks and the latest successful check.

Click an individual figure, not only the card, to open the corresponding pre-filtered list. Select Refresh dashboard after major imports when an immediate refresh is needed.

docs.title: 3. Device Management
docs.text:
Figure: Device list and device-management workspace.

The device list can be filtered by name, tenant, site, group and status. Depending on permission, the toolbar and context menu allow you to:

  1. create, edit or duplicate a device;
  2. check status for one device or the current result set;
  3. open the embedded or standard browser interface;
  4. retrieve system information, remote files and the website log;
  5. deactivate or archive a device with confirmation.

The lower detail pages show master data, files, status history and audit. Remote file downloads use the configured destination and never alter the archive until the normal import path processes them.

docs.title: 4. Sources and download tasks
docs.text:
Open Administration → Sources to configure FTP or DLT API endpoints. Test the connection before assigning a source to a task. Credentials are protected and do not appear in logs.

Figure: Administration of FTP and DLT API sources.

Open Administration → Tasks to define source selection, file patterns, recursion, schedule, archive behaviour and optional strict cloud-backup mode. Run now opens a detailed progress view with one row per source and live log messages. RFID terminal CSV files require RFID_*.CSV in the pattern.

Figure: Scheduled and manually executable download tasks.

In strict mode, a terminal file is not marked as processed or deleted until its cloud copy is verified.

docs.title: 5. Tachodata Archive
docs.text:
Figure: Filterable C/M tachodata archive with status columns.

The archive contains driver-card C files and vehicle-unit M files. Filter by date, name, driver/vehicle, file type, generation, parser status, signature status, deletion state or backup state.

docs.title: Import and status
docs.text:
• Import DDD files accepts one or more local files.
  • Content determines C/M type; the filename is only a hint.
  • Successful parsing stores structured details without changing the original archive object.
  • VALID means all available signatures and the chain could be verified.
  • INVALID or UNVERIFIABLE places the file in quarantine; it remains viewable and exportable but cannot drive trusted links or combined evaluations.

docs.title: Open, export and delete
docs.text:
• Double-click or choose Open analysis to open the C or M analysis.
  • One selected row exports the original DDD; several rows or the current view export a ZIP.
  • Logical deletion requires a reason and is recorded in audit. It does not automatically delete a verified cloud backup.
  • Details / Signature / Audit explains hashes, parser warnings, signature sections and history.

docs.title: Backup status
docs.text: Verified is shown only after complete upload and remote hash verification. Use the context menu to retry, verify or restore a file. Backup errors are also linked from the dashboard.

docs.title: 6. Driver-card analysis
docs.text:
The header identifies the driver, card number and selected archive file. The analysis always uses the selected C file as its base.

Figure: Driver-card analysis for one selected C archive.

Tab Purpose Overview identity, validity, issuing authorities, generation and data range Activities sortable intervals with UTC timestamps and C/M availability Day / Week graphical timelines, totals and details for the selected period DDD week view Web browser-style interactive week view using the selected file Events / Faults technical records from the card Vehicles used vehicle identifiers observed on the card Positions location list and interactive map Violations technical EU 561/2006 pre-check Signatures chain and per-EF verification results

The Data availability filter distinguishes all activities, C and M available, driver card only and vehicle unit only. The other source is comparison evidence; it never replaces the selected base file. All displayed tachograph calculations use UTC.

docs.title: 7. Vehicle-unit analysis
docs.text:
Figure: Vehicle-unit analysis for one selected M archive.

The M analysis shows vehicle identity and the selected M file. Driver sessions are grouped by normalised card number and generation. Team driving displays slot 1 and slot 2 separately.

Tabs include drivers, activities, day/week timelines, events/faults, speed, technical data, calibrations, positions/borders/loading, signatures and violations. Technical data uses a field tree; Calibrations lists calibration time, next calibration, workshop, VIN and registration. Position tabs can open the interactive map.

“No assigned driver card” means the M file contains slot activity for which no active card-insertion interval with a usable card number was found. It does not mean that a matching C archive is merely missing; the tooltip in day/week views explains this distinction.

docs.title: 8. Fleet Management
docs.text:
The left icon navigation opens non-duplicated work tabs. A new session starts with an empty fleet workspace.

Figure: Fleet Management icon navigation and non-duplicated work tabs.

docs.title: Master data
docs.text:
• People: personal number, identity, contact data, licence data, FSK policy and status.
  • Vehicles: internal number, registration, VIN, type and status.
  • Due types: system and user-defined deadline categories for people or vehicles.
  • Suppliers / Cost categories: finance master data.

Figure: Personnel master-data list.

Figure: Vehicle master-data list.

Double-click a person or vehicle to open its record. Context menus jump directly to deadlines, tachodata, RFID or finance pages.

docs.title: Person record
docs.text:
Figure: Person record with related business tabs.

The record contains master data, deadlines, licence checks, RFID history, card links/files, day/week/activity views and audit. Use Assign RFID/NFC to read a UID from a local PC/SC reader. FSK via PC/SC records a successful check only when the read UID belongs to the selected person.

docs.title: Vehicle record
docs.text:
Figure: Vehicle record with tachograph, finance and position tabs.

Tabs include deadlines, costs/credits, foreign invoices, tachograph links, M files, drivers/activities, events/faults, speed, technical data, calibrations, positions and audit. Position rows can be opened together on the map.

docs.title: 9. Deadlines and download due dates
docs.text:
Personnel and vehicle deadline evaluations are separate but use the same sortable/filterable component. Every deadline has an individual lead time; the default is ten days. Colours mean:

  • green: outside the lead time;
  • blue/orange status: approaching or due today;
  • red: overdue;
  • completed: closed with completion date.

Automatic download deadlines are managed by the system:

Figure: Filterable personnel deadline evaluation.

  • current driver card: readout date + 28 days;
  • vehicle unit matched by VIN: readout date + 90 days.

The readout date is taken from a valid C_YYYYMMDD_HHmm... or M_YYYYMMDD_HHmm... filename, then falls back to source creation, download and archive UTC dates. Automatic deadlines cannot be manually moved or completed. Tachograph calibration remains a separate vehicle deadline.

docs.title: 10. Driving-licence checks and RFID
docs.text:
The Personnel navigation provides:

Figure: Driving-licence-check (FSK) due-date evaluation.

  • FSK evaluation with interval, last check, next due date, terminal and status;
  • licence-check history;
  • RFID pending list for unknown UIDs or invalid CSV rows.

The default check interval is 28 days. Successful PC/SC, terminal CSV and resolved mobile records use the same service and update one managed FSK deadline. Assigning an RFID UID alone is not a check.

Unknown terminal UIDs remain pending. Assigning them creates the effective check; discarding requires a reason and never creates a permanent RFID assignment.

docs.title: 11. Notifications
docs.text:
Under Administration → Notifications, configure SMTP, responsible recipients, rules/templates and the send log. The system is disabled until SMTP and recipients have been tested and sending is explicitly enabled.

Figure: Notification rules, templates and schedules.

Standard rules cover weekly information, daily reminders from ten days, urgent reminders from three days, overdue warnings and a daily responsible-person report. Rules may be previewed or sent manually when the user has permission.

docs.title: Direct vehicle costs
docs.text:
Open a vehicle record → Costs / Credits. Create a cost or credit, choose a cost category and enter document date, net amount and tax rate. Amounts are stored in cents and displayed right-aligned. Direct entries may be verified and booked without an original document if policy permits.

Figure: Supplier master data for financial documents.

docs.title: Foreign invoices
docs.text:
Open Fleet → Foreign invoices. Double-click an invoice to open it as a work tab.

Figure: Foreign-invoice list and work-tab entry point.

flowchart LR
    A[Create draft header] --> B[Add vehicle positions]
    B --> C[Upload original documents]
    C --> D[Cloud hash verified]
    D --> E[Verify totals]
    E --> F[Book]
    F --> G[REST or CSV/ZIP accounting export]

The status row compares gross invoice amount, split amount and difference. Zero difference is green; any cent difference is red and blocks verification. Invoice-bound positions inherit the invoice date for document/valid-from/valid-to and are always variable. Booked data is immutable; corrections use cancellation or linked credits.

docs.title: 13. Audit, reports and logs
docs.text:
The global audit under Administration → Audit combines device, tachograph and fleet events. Filter by date, user, action or text. Double-click an entry to open its current related object when the stable ID can be resolved. Navigation itself is not audited.

Figure: Global audit log with object-based navigation.

Deadline and evaluation grids support print preview, system printing and PDF. Only visible filtered rows and columns are included.

Administration → Logs shows app.log, REST logs and native crash diagnostics. Logs use English technical messages regardless of the selected UI language.

docs.title: 14. Tray and shutdown
docs.text: Closing the main window normally keeps background services running in the system tray. Use the tray action to restore the window. Use System → Exit application and services or the equivalent tray command for a clean shutdown; schedulers, REST API, notification service, backup coordinator and logs are closed in order.

docs.title: 15. Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Symptom Action File is quarantined Open Details/Signatures; install missing crypto/trust material or verify the source. Do not re-label the file manually. Backup remains pending Test the active target, inspect quota/authentication and use Back up now. No PC/SC reader Install the reader driver, reconnect it and reopen the read dialog. No C/M comparison Verify matching card number, generation, UTC range and valid signature status. Invoice cannot be verified Resolve split difference and ensure at least one original document is cloud-verified. UI job failed Use Retry, then inspect app.log with the displayed job/request ID.

For administrator-level recovery, continue with the Administrator and integrator guide.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text: The accounting adapter transmits a booked foreign invoice, supplier, vehicle positions and original documents to a configured HTTPS endpoint. FLEET Mira is the HTTP client; the accounting system implements the receiver.

docs.title: 2. Preconditions
docs.text:
• invoice status is BOOKED;
  • header totals match active positions to the cent;
  • at least one original document is cloud-verified;
  • user has finance.export;
  • endpoint uses HTTPS;
  • no successful export exists for the same idempotency key.

docs.title: 3. Request
docs.text:
POST /configured/accounting/path HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=...
Idempotency-Key: <stable-sha256>

Multipart fields:

Field Content type Meaning metadata application/json; charset=utf-8 complete versioned accounting bundle document_1...n original MIME type byte-identical, hash-verified originals

docs.title: 4. Metadata model
docs.text:
Top level:

Field Meaning format technical constant DLTNG-ACCOUNTING retained for compatibility version contract version invoice invoice header supplier supplier master snapshot positions vehicle-linked cost/credit positions

Important invoice fields include stable ID, document type/number/date, delivery date/number, cost centre, due date, payment/discount terms, net/tax/gross cents, status and currency. Supplier includes stable ID, supplier/creditor numbers, address, tax ID, phone and email. Positions include stable ID, vehicle ID/number/registration/VIN, category, type, description, validity/document dates, net/tax/gross cents, tax rate basis points and variable flag.

Amounts are integer cents. Tax rates are integer basis points (1900 = 19.00%). Credits are identified by type; stored amounts remain positive.

docs.title: 5. Idempotency
docs.text: The key is deterministic for invoice ID, booked revision and export contract version. A receiver must return the original successful result for a repeated key and must not post twice.

docs.title: 6. Response
docs.text:
Success (200 or 201):

{
  "status": "accepted",
  "external_id": "ACC-2026-004712",
  "message": "Imported"
}

external_id is persisted with response time. A non-2xx response keeps the outbox item retryable and stores a sanitised error. Authentication bodies/secrets are not audited.

docs.title: 7. Process
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant M as FLEET Mira
    participant V as Document vault
    participant A as Accounting endpoint
    U->>M: Transfer booked invoice
    M->>V: Download and hash-check originals
    M->>M: Build bundle and idempotency key
    M->>A: HTTPS multipart POST
    alt accepted
        A-->>M: external_id
        M->>M: Mark export successful and audit
    else temporary failure
        A-->>M: error
        M->>M: Persist outbox retry
    end

docs.title: 8. Receiver requirements
docs.text:
• enforce HTTPS and authenticated clients;
  • limit body size and accepted MIME types;
  • stream originals and verify declared hashes where exposed;
  • implement atomic invoice/document handling;
  • persist idempotency keys;
  • return stable external IDs;
  • redact credentials and document contents from logs.

docs.title: 9. Error handling
docs.text: Status Receiver meaning 400/422 permanent contract/validation error; operator correction required 401/403 credentials/permission error 409 idempotency or business conflict; return existing result when equivalent 413 payload exceeds receiver limit 429 retry later; honour Retry-After 5xx transient receiver failure; outbox retry

docs.title: 10. Compatibility
docs.text: Fields are additive within a contract version. Removing/renaming fields or changing amount semantics requires a new version. The legacy DLTNG-ACCOUNTING format token is deliberately not rebranded.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
The DriverCard API exposes the same parsed driver-card data used by the FLEET Mira desktop analysis. It is intended for local integration and the bundled web week view. It does not modify archived originals.

Default base URL: http://127.0.0.1:5055. Start/stop/open/log commands are available under System. Binding beyond loopback requires an external TLS/authentication gateway and an explicit risk assessment.

docs.title: 2. Time, formats and filters
docs.text:
• timestamps are ISO 8601 UTC;
  • dates are YYYY-MM-DD;
  • durations are integer seconds unless stated otherwise;
  • card numbers are normalised strings;
  • JSON uses UTF-8;
  • validation errors return JSON with error and a readable message.

Common query parameters:

Parameter Meaning import_id parsed import identifier archive_id stable tachodata archive identifier card_number exact normalised card number date_from, date_to inclusive UTC date range limit, offset bounded pagination where supported

docs.title: 3. Endpoints
docs.text: Method and path Function GET /health process and database readiness POST /api/drivercard/parse parse a supplied DDD payload and persist/reuse its import GET /api/drivercard/imports list parsed driver-card imports GET /api/drivercard/time-summary activity totals by type GET /api/drivercard/activities activity intervals GET /api/drivercard/events card events GET /api/drivercard/faults card faults GET /api/drivercard/vehicles-used vehicles recorded on the card GET /api/drivercard/positions recorded positions GET /api/drivercard/day-overview prepared day data GET /api/drivercard/week-overview prepared ISO-week data GET /api/drivercard/eu561/violations technical EU 561/2006 pre-check

docs.title: 4. Health
docs.text:
GET /health HTTP/1.1
Host: 127.0.0.1:5055

{"status":"ok","service":"drivercard-rest","database":"available"}

docs.title: 5. Parse a driver card
docs.text:
POST /api/drivercard/parse accepts JSON. The desktop web preload uses base64 so no local archive path appears in the browser URL.

{
  "file_name": "C_20250715_0615_Demo_1000000000000001.DDD",
  "content_base64": "BASE64_DATA",
  "archive_id": "optional-stable-id"
}

The response identifies import/archive, driver/card metadata, activity range and parser warnings. Binary input, signatures and certificates are never echoed. A repeated identical payload is idempotent by hash.

docs.title: 6. Activities and summaries
docs.text:
GET /api/drivercard/activities?archive_id=ARCHIVE_ID&date_from=2025-07-14&date_to=2025-07-20

Activity rows contain start/end UTC, activity code (DRIVING, WORK, AVAILABILITY, REST), duration, card, crew and slot information. Day/week endpoints return the same source intervals grouped for visualisation; they are not separate evidence records.

docs.title: 7. Events, faults, vehicles and positions
docs.text: Event/fault responses include technical type/code, start/end and readable description where known. Unknown standard extensions remain represented by the technical code. Position rows contain timestamp, latitude, longitude, source/type, country/region codes and optional odometer.

docs.title: 8. EU 561/2006 pre-check
docs.text: The endpoint evaluates only the selected data basis. Its response includes driver summary, violations, warnings, rule/algorithm version and data-gap notices. It is a technical pre-check, not an authority/legal decision. National exceptions, AETR and Article 12 circumstances are not decided automatically.

docs.title: 9. HTTP status codes
docs.text: Status Meaning 200 successful query or idempotent parse 400 malformed payload/filter 404 archive/import not found 409 conflicting or quarantined data for the requested trusted operation 413 payload too large for configured limit 422 DDD content cannot be parsed 500 unexpected internal failure; correlate with REST log

docs.title: 10. Security and operation
docs.text:
• Keep loopback binding unless a protected integration gateway is deployed.
  • Do not put DDD data or local paths in query strings.
  • Restrict REST log access; logs are English and exclude raw DDD/secrets.
  • Stop the API through FLEET Mira before maintenance.
  • API paths and response field names retain their technical compatibility even though the visible product is FLEET Mira.

docs.title: Driver-card analysis
docs.text: The analysis is always bound to one selected C file and archive ID. M data is comparison evidence only. Quarantined files remain viewable but cannot feed an effective combined evaluation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Figure: Card identity, validity, authorities, generation and data range.

The header identifies the anonymised driver, card and file. A quarantine banner explains missing or invalid signature verification. Empty values mean that the selected file does not contain the information or it could not be read safely.

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
Figure: Sortable UTC intervals with C/M availability.

Data availability filters all intervals, C and M available, C only or M only. C remains the base. Rows include start, end, duration, activity, card, slot and counterpart archives; double-click and context actions open unambiguous sources.

docs.title: Day chart
docs.text:
Figure: One UTC day with driving, work, availability and rest/break.

The date, arrow buttons and Go to change the visible day. The availability track indicates C/M coverage, while activity tracks show business intervals. The detail grid remains sortable.

docs.title: Week chart
docs.text:
Figure: ISO week from Monday to Sunday.

Seven UTC days are shown together. Empty days remain visible and are not interpreted as activity. Totals and tooltips refer to the selected file and visible period.

docs.title: DDD week view Web
docs.text:
Figure: Secure in-app entry point to the browser-based week view.

The page starts the local web view through a tokenised hand-off; local archive paths do not appear in its URL. Documentation deliberately does not load a signed original DDD into an external page.

docs.title: Events and faults
docs.text:
Figure: Technical event and fault records.

Codes, periods, vehicles and descriptions come from the selected card. An empty page means no matching record exists; it does not mean records were removed.

docs.title: Vehicles used
docs.text:
Figure: Vehicle references with usage range and odometer values.

Only vehicles reported by this C file are listed. A vehicle-record link is offered only for an unambiguous master-data match.

docs.title: Positions
docs.text:
Figure: PLACE and GNSS records with nation, region and coordinates.

Open position map displays visible coordinates. Nation and region are shown as readable text plus technical value where possible. See Position maps.

docs.title: Violations
docs.text:
Figure: Reproducible technical pre-check for the selected C file.

The list explains rule, article, actual/target value, deviation and legal basis. It does not replace legal assessment of national exemptions, AETR or Article 12 cases. Saving a snapshot requires archive.manage; export requires archive.export.

docs.title: Signatures
docs.text:
Figure: Certificate chain and signed-EF results.

VALID confirms a verified chain and all present signatures. INVALID and UNVERIFIABLE quarantine the file; the unchanged archive object remains available for evidence-preserving export.

docs.title: Foreign invoices
docs.text: A foreign invoice opens as a work tab. Its status strip shows workflow, document and export states plus gross total, split amount and difference. Green means an exact cent-level match; red means an unresolved difference.

docs.title: Positions
docs.text:
Figure: Vehicle allocation and totals status.

Every position belongs to exactly one vehicle. Document date, valid from/to and Variable are inherited from the header and locked for invoice positions. Money is right-aligned; costs increase and credits reduce the split amount.

docs.title: Original documents
docs.text:
Figure: Version, filename, origin, cloud state and retention.

The original remains byte-identical in the cloud document vault. Back up documents now uploads pending files, reads them back and verifies SHA-256. Preview uses a temporary copy that is safely cleaned after the external viewer closes. Verification is blocked until an original is cloud-verified.

docs.title: Export history
docs.text:
Figure: Adapter, state, transfer time, external ID and error.

Only booked or cancelled invoices may be transferred by accounting REST or CSV/ZIP. Idempotency keys prevent duplicate transfers; technical failures remain visible for retry.

docs.title: Audit
docs.text:
Figure: Chronological, immutable business history.

Creation, edits, verification, booking, cancellation, document verification and export are traceable. Navigation and preview do not create a new audit record.

docs.title: Workflow and permissions
docs.text:
1. Enter header and vehicle positions in Draft.
  2. Attach and cloud-verify the original document.
  3. Bring the difference to 0.00 and Verify.
  4. Use finance.book to Book immutably.
  5. Optionally export through accounting REST or CSV/ZIP.

Booked records are not edited or deleted. Corrections use cancellation or a linked credit. Reading requires finance.read, editing finance.manage, verification finance.verify, booking finance.book, and export finance.export.

docs.title: Position maps
docs.text: Position maps display archived coordinates interactively. Viewing a map changes no business data and creates no audit event.

docs.title: Driver card
docs.text: Figure: C positions on OpenStreetMap with source and type filters.

docs.title: Vehicle unit
docs.text: Figure: M positions, border and loading records on OpenStreetMap.

docs.title: Operation and interpretation
docs.text:
• + / − zoom; drag to pan; All points restores the complete extent.
  • Source filters toggle PLACE, GNSS-3H and other records.
  • Type filters distinguish begin, RFU, three-hour GNSS, border and loading records where present.
  • Marker colours follow the legend. Click or hover to see time, source, type, driver/card or vehicle reference and available location text.
  • Filters affect display only; the archive object and position list remain unchanged.

The map uses OpenStreetMap tiles and displays the required attribution. Building the view requires connectivity to the configured tile endpoint; tiles may subsequently come from the local cache. A missing location name is not a missing point: coordinates can exist even when nation or region cannot be resolved unambiguously.

docs.title: Personnel and fleet tachodata evaluations
docs.text: The two overview workspaces group archived tachograph files by person or vehicle. They are the entry point to single-file analysis and show known coverage gaps and the next download due date. All times are UTC.

docs.title: Personnel
docs.text:
Figure: Personnel overview with download due date and data coverage.

Each parent row shows driver, personnel number, file count, earliest and latest data timestamp, next download and gap status. Search covers person, card, filename and archive ID. Date, gap and file-status filters can be combined.

Figure: Expanded driver-card files and archive coverage gaps.

Tachograph files lists linked C files with card number, archive date, period, filename, size, task, status, archive ID and path. Archive coverage gaps lists uncovered intervals between known data ranges. No gap is invented before the first or after the last range unless a business target period exists.

docs.title: Fleet
docs.text:
Figure: Vehicle overview by registration and internal number.

The fleet view works the same way, but assigns M files through the confirmed vehicle link or VIN. Generation, parser and signature state remain visible per file.

Figure: Expanded M file and calculated vehicle data coverage.

docs.title: Due date and operation
docs.text:
• Green: outside the individual lead time.
  • Orange: within the default ten-day lead time.
  • Red: download due date has passed.
  • A dash means that no effective, unambiguous download due date can be determined yet.

Double-click a file to open the central driver-card or vehicle-unit analysis in one reusable work tab. The context menu offers analysis, archive record, single/selection export and ID/path copy. Export requires archive.export; viewing requires archive.read. Column layout, sorting, filters and expansion state are stored per user.

docs.title: Vehicle-unit analysis
docs.text: Vehicle-unit analysis uses one selected M file as its base. Drivers, slots and card sessions remain separate; C files supplement comparison only within proven card insertion periods.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Figure: Generation, signature, vehicle identity, data range and parser warnings.

Warnings identify optional or incomplete download sections. Missing second-by-second speed data, for example, does not change the preserved M file.

docs.title: Drivers
docs.text:
Figure: Insertion/withdrawal, slot, card, generation and name.

“No assigned driver card” means no usable active card-insertion interval was found for the slot and period. It does not merely mean that a C file is absent; timeline tooltips explain this distinction.

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
Figure: Vehicle activity intervals with data availability.

Driver changes and team driving are separated by card, generation and slot. Parallel activities from two drivers are never collapsed into one driver row.

docs.title: Day chart
docs.text:
Figure: Driver groups, insertion period and activity tracks for one UTC day.

Navigation depends only on the date. Driver and card appear in group headings. Tooltips on unknown-card groups explain their evidence basis.

docs.title: Week chart
docs.text:
Figure: One ISO week with separate driver panels.

Each calendar week appears once. Multiple sessions for the same driver are combined in that weekly panel; driver changes and team drivers stay separate.

docs.title: Events and faults
docs.text: Figure: Records with code, description, start, end and section.

docs.title: Speed
docs.text:
Figure: Second-by-second speed or a documented empty state.

Only speed records present in the file are shown. An empty state is never replaced by calculated or synthetic data.

docs.title: Technical data
docs.text:
Figure: Readable field tree for manufacturer, device, software and approval.

The tree retains its download-section reference. Unknown optional records remain available through a bounded raw reference.

docs.title: Calibrations
docs.text:
Figure: Calibration time, next calibration, purpose, workshop, VIN and registration.

When a vehicle is created, FLEET Mira uses the chronologically latest suitable record for the separate calibration deadline.

docs.title: Positions, borders and loading
docs.text:
Figure: Time and location records from the selected M file.

The list combines GNSS positions, border crossings and loading/unloading without losing the record type. Coordinate-bearing rows can be opened together on the map.

docs.title: Signatures
docs.text: Figure: Verification results for signed download sections.

docs.title: Violations
docs.text:
Figure: Per-driver pre-check and data-coverage notes.

Selected M file checks trips on this vehicle only. Full driver history may supplement other trusted C/M archives. Unknown-card intervals are reported as notes, not personal violations.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text: The CSV/ZIP export is a transport-neutral hand-off for booked foreign invoices. One ZIP contains the invoice header, supplier snapshot, all vehicle positions, a manifest and every verified original document.

docs.title: 2. Foreign-invoice workflow in FLEET Mira
docs.text:
1. Create the supplier and cost categories.
  2. Create an invoice draft with number/date, totals and payment details.
  3. Add one or more vehicle-linked cost/credit positions.
  4. Add received originals; the document vault uploads and verifies each hash.
  5. Resolve the gross/split difference to exactly zero cents.
  6. Verify and book the invoice.
  7. Export once or transfer through REST. Corrections use cancellation/credit, never edits to booked rows.
flowchart LR
    H[Invoice header] --> P[Vehicle positions]
    P --> D[Original documents]
    D --> V[Cloud verification]
    V --> C[Cent-accurate validation]
    C --> B[Booking]
    B --> Z[CSV/ZIP export]

docs.title: 3. Archive layout
docs.text:
Filename: Accounting_<document-number>_<invoice-id>.zip (filesystem-invalid characters are replaced).

manifest.json
invoice.csv
supplier.csv
positions.csv
documents/
  <document-id>_<original-file-name>

CSV is UTF-8 with BOM and semicolon delimiter. The first row contains stable field names. Line endings are CRLF. Values containing delimiter, quotes or line breaks are RFC-4180 quoted.

docs.title: 4. manifest.json
docs.text: Field Meaning format DLTNG-ACCOUNTING-EXPORT legacy contract token version export format version idempotency_key stable duplicate-prevention key invoice_id FLEET Mira invoice UUID documents[] ordered archive names of original documents

docs.title: 5. invoice.csv
docs.text:
Exactly one data row.

Field Meaning/function id immutable invoice UUID document_type INVOICE or CREDIT_NOTE correction_of_invoice_id linked corrected invoice, optional document_number supplier invoice/credit number document_date ISO document date delivery_date ISO performance/delivery date, optional delivery_note_number supplier delivery note, optional cost_center accounting cost centre, optional payable_until due date payment_term_days net payment term discount_term_days cash-discount term discount_rate_bp discount basis points net_cents declared invoice net amount tax_cents declared tax amount gross_cents calculated net + tax currency currently EUR status must be BOOKED booked_at_utc, booked_by booking evidence created_at_utc, updated_at_utc technical history description, note optional business text

docs.title: 6. supplier.csv
docs.text:
Exactly one snapshot row.

Field Meaning/function id supplier UUID supplier_number internal supplier number name supplier name creditor_number accounting creditor number street, postal_code, city, country postal address tax_id tax/VAT identifier phone, email validated contact data payment_term_days supplier default

The row is the current master snapshot at export time; the invoice ID and originals remain the audit anchor.

docs.title: 7. positions.csv
docs.text:
One row per active invoice position.

Field Meaning/function id position UUID invoice_id owning invoice UUID vehicle_id vehicle UUID vehicle_internal_number operational fleet number vehicle_registration registration snapshot vehicle_vin VIN snapshot cost_category_id, cost_category_code, cost_category_name cost classification entry_type COST or CREDIT description booking text valid_from, valid_to, document_date invoice date for invoice-bound positions net_cents, tax_cents, gross_cents cent amounts tax_rate_bp VAT basis points variable always true for invoice-bound positions note optional text

Signed comparison amount is cost positive and credit negative. For a credit-note header the comparison normalises the invoice sign so the displayed difference remains meaningful.

docs.title: 8. Original documents
docs.text: Only cloud-verified originals are included. Bytes must match the stored SHA-256; generated previews never replace XML or hybrid invoice originals. The ZIP itself is a delivery package, not the retention master. The document vault remains the evidence store.

docs.title: 9. Validation rules
docs.text:
• gross_cents = net_cents + tax_cents;
  • signed sum of active position gross equals the invoice gross under document-type sign rules;
  • every position references exactly one vehicle;
  • every manifest document exists in documents/;
  • duplicate supplier/document number/date or identical document hash requires administrative override before booking;
  • only one successful export per idempotency key.

docs.title: 10. Audit and import guidance
docs.text: FLEET Mira audits export start/result, invoice, document IDs and correlation ID but not document content. Importers should validate manifest version, reject path traversal, process to a transaction/staging area, persist the idempotency key and return a reconciliation report containing invoice ID and target accounting ID.

docs.title: 11. Compatibility
docs.text: Consumers must ignore unknown additive columns. Required column removal, rename, type or sign change creates a new format version. The DLTNG-... format string remains a legacy technical identifier.

docs.title: 1. Scope
docs.text: FLEET Mira parses driver-card C files and vehicle-unit M files through one TachographImportService. The original compressed archive object remains the evidence source; parsed tables are replaceable derived data.

docs.title: 2. Import pipeline
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[Archive ID] --> B[Read and verify GZIP/hash]
    B --> C[Classify binary content]
    C --> D[Parse C or M structure]
    D --> E[Verify certificates and signed sections]
    E --> F{Result}
    F -->|Valid| G[Atomically replace derived rows]
    F -->|Invalid/unverifiable| H[Persist quarantine results]
    F -->|Technical failure| I[Keep previous valid details]
    G --> J[Links, deadlines, UI cache invalidation]
    H --> J

Public integration points:

TachographImportService(db_path, logger).import_archive_id(archive_id)
DriverCardFullParser.parse_bytes(data)
VehicleUnitParser.parse(path)
DriverCardSignatureVerificationService.verify(parsed, trust_store, reference_time)
SignatureVerificationService.verify_sections(parsed, trust_store)

docs.title: 3. Classification
docs.text: Classification uses protocol/file identifiers in the binary content, never the filename alone. Result values are DRIVER_CARD, VEHICLE_UNIT and UNKNOWN. C/M filename prefixes are validated only for readout-date and plausibility functions.

docs.title: 4. Driver-card data
docs.text:
The C parser preserves block kind, application/file ID, generation, order, raw data bytes and following signature bytes. It maps identity, card validity, authorities, driving-licence number, activities, events, faults, vehicles and positions.

Important identity mapping:

Parser value Business use holder surname/first names person conflict check and display birth date optional person value card number/generation card link and C/M matching card issue/valid-from/expiry card record and expiry deadline driving-licence number/authority person licence data preferred language/country card metadata

No guessed values are generated from unrelated byte ranges. Text fields strip encoding indicators/control bytes and normalise whitespace without changing meaningful characters.

docs.title: 5. Vehicle-unit data
docs.text:
Supported download groups are G1 TREP 01–05, G2 v1 21–25 and G2 v2 31–35. Derived tables include overview/identity, card sessions, slot activities, events, faults, overspeed, second-by-second speed, technical/calibration records and position/border/loading data.

Unknown bounded optional records are retained as warnings. Invalid length/protocol sequences produce parse failure. Driver/workshop names use the same normalisation rules as C text.

docs.title: C files
docs.text:
• G1: EF.Card_Certificate, EF.CA_Certificate, RSA PKCS#1 and SHA-1 over exact data-block bytes.
  • G2/G2V2: Card_Sign, MSCA_Card, optional link certificate and ECDSA over exact signed EFs.

docs.title: M files
docs.text:
Every available signed VU download section is verified separately against the applicable ERCA/MSCA/VU chain. Trust and certificate validity use the best plausible signed file time, otherwise archive/download time with warning.

Overall priority: INVALID, then UNVERIFIABLE, then VALID. cryptography missing, signature/certificate missing or trust anchor missing produces UNVERIFIABLE, not VALID.

docs.title: 7. Parser and quarantine status
docs.text:
Status Meaning PARSED_VERIFIED parse and all required verification valid PARSED_VERIFIED_WITH_WARNINGS valid evidence with non-fatal parser warnings QUARANTINED_INVALID_SIGNATURE cryptographic invalidity QUARANTINED_UNVERIFIABLE required proof cannot be completed PARSE_ERROR structural/technical parse failure

Quarantine files remain visible/exportable but cannot create trusted person/vehicle links, contribute to combined C/M analysis or compliance snapshots.

docs.title: 8. C/M comparison
docs.text: The selected archive remains the base. Opposite-source intervals match by normalised card number, card generation and UTC overlap. A sweep-line comparison cuts only on interval boundaries. Valid overlapping data is shown as C and M available; one-sided coverage remains C-only or M-only. C data has priority only in the complete person-history view, never by modifying raw source rows.

docs.title: 9. Reprocessing and idempotency
docs.text: Archive hash prevents duplicate originals. Reprocessing writes new derived rows only after a successful parse/verification transaction. A failed reparse preserves the last valid details. Import, deletion, restore or reparse invalidates affected UI comparison caches.

docs.title: 10. Extending the parser
docs.text:
When adding a record type:

  1. document normative file/record identifiers and generation;
  2. validate lengths before reading;
  3. preserve source section/index and raw reference;
  4. keep UTC values unchanged;
  5. add fixture and malformed-length tests;
  6. add signature impact tests;
  7. update both language versions of this document and API schemas;
  8. never infer a business identity from an ambiguous value.

The older technical module and table names containing ddd or DLTNG remain compatibility identifiers.

docs.title: 1. Scope and responsibility
docs.text:
FLEET Mira backs up immutable tachograph archive objects and consistent SQLite snapshots to one active Google Drive or HTTPS WebDAV target. The local archive remains primary. Backup is one-way; remote changes are never synchronised back automatically.

Financial originals use the same active provider infrastructure but a separate financial-documents/... namespace and retention rules.

docs.title: 2. Remote layout
docs.text:
DLTNG/<installation-id>/
  archives/YYYY/MM/<archive-id>.ddd.gz[.enc]
  database/YYYY/MM/archive_YYYY-MM-DD_HHmmssZ.db.gz[.enc]
  manifests/...
  financial-documents/YYYY/<invoice-id>/<document-id>_<sha256>.<ext>[.enc]

DLTNG is the compatibility storage prefix and is not renamed to FLEET Mira. Renaming it would orphan existing objects.

docs.title: 3. Status model
docs.text:
Status Meaning Not configured no active target Pending durable upload job exists Uploading provider transfer in progress Verified remote object has expected size/hash Failed retryable/permanent error recorded Missing remotely previously verified object no longer exists Retained after local deletion remote DDD retained by policy

The archive list shows “backup present” only for Verified.

docs.title: 4. Target setup
docs.text:
Open Administration → Backups:

  1. choose Google Drive or WebDAV;
  2. authenticate/select target folder;
  3. keep client-side encryption enabled unless policy explicitly permits plaintext;
  4. upload/read back a random test object;
  5. export and safely store the recovery key;
  6. confirm the recovery-key procedure;
  7. activate target and start initial database backup;
  8. monitor initial archive queue until verified.

docs.title: WebDAV
docs.text: Provide HTTPS URL, username, app password and root folder. HTTP is rejected. The test performs OPTIONS, PROPFIND, collection creation, upload and read-back. Upload prefers temporary object + MOVE; if unsupported, direct upload is not published as verified until full read-back succeeds.

docs.title: Google Drive
docs.text: Use the published FLEET Mira desktop OAuth client or import an approved organisation client definition. Scope is limited to drive.file. My Drive and Shared Drives are supported. Refresh tokens are DPAPI-protected. Uploads are resumable.

docs.title: 5. Encryption
docs.text:
Client-side encryption uses AES-256-GCM with authenticated metadata. The target key is DPAPI-protected locally. The exported recovery-key file is the disaster-recovery dependency and must be stored offline/separately.

Never store the recovery key only in the same cloud target. Losing both Windows DPAPI context and recovery key makes encrypted backups unrecoverable.

docs.title: 6. Archive backup flow
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[Local archive transaction] --> B[Archive row + backup job committed]
    B --> C[Compute/confirm compressed SHA-256]
    C --> D[Optional AES-GCM encryption]
    D --> E[Upload temporary/resumable object]
    E --> F[Remote stat/read-back]
    F --> G{Size and hash match?}
    G -->|Yes| H[Verified status + audit]
    G -->|No| I[Failed/conflict + retry]

Retries occur after 1, 5, 15 and 60 minutes and then no more often than every six hours. A process restart resumes durable pending jobs. Same archive ID with different remote content is a conflict and is never overwritten silently.

docs.title: 7. Strict terminal-deletion mode
docs.text: Normally local archiving completes the download and cloud backup follows asynchronously. A task may enable Wait for cloud backup before deleting from terminal. In strict mode, mark_processed and deletion are deferred until Verified. A later run recognises the local duplicate, continues its pending backup and deletes the terminal object only after verification.

docs.title: 8. Database snapshots
docs.text:
Default schedule: daily 02:00 UTC, with same-day catch-up after application restart.

  1. create a consistent file using SQLite Backup API;
  2. run PRAGMA integrity_check;
  3. hash SHA-256;
  4. GZIP compress;
  5. optionally encrypt;
  6. upload and verify remotely.

Retention keeps the latest 30 daily snapshots and one monthly snapshot for 12 months. Only expired database snapshots are automatically removed; DDD archive objects are never automatically deleted.

docs.title: 9. Restore one archive object
docs.text:
1. Select the archive row and choose Restore from backup.
  2. Confirm administrative permission and reason where required.
  3. Download to a temporary file.
  4. Verify remote size/hash, decrypt and verify authenticated data.
  5. Verify compressed and decompressed hashes.
  6. Atomically move to the expected local archive path.
  7. Reconcile status; do not automatically re-activate a logically deleted business row.

Failure leaves the current local object untouched and removes temporary partial data.

docs.title: 10. Database restore
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[Select remote snapshot] --> B[Download to temporary location]
    B --> C[Hash/decrypt/decompress]
    C --> D[SQLite integrity_check]
    D --> E[Stop schedulers and background writers]
    E --> F[Create safety copy of current DB]
    F --> G[Atomically replace database]
    G --> H[Restart application and run migrations]
    H --> I[Reconcile archive/backup state]

Database replacement is staged for application restart when the live connection cannot be safely closed. If preflight validation fails, no replacement request is committed.

docs.title: 11. Full disaster recovery
docs.text:
1. Install the same or compatible FLEET Mira version on an isolated host/path.
  2. Configure provider access without starting normal imports.
  3. Supply the recovery key if encrypted.
  4. restore and validate the selected database snapshot;
  5. start once, apply compatible migrations and stop background writes;
  6. enumerate manifests and restore all missing archive/document objects;
  7. verify compressed/decompressed archive hashes and document SHA-256;
  8. reconcile database links, backup status, retention and audit;
  9. test representative C/M analysis, user sign-in and finance originals;
  10. document approval before production cutover.

docs.title: 12. Target change
docs.text: A target cannot be deleted while documents reference it. Use the target-change workflow to copy every retained object to the new target, read back/verify it and update references only after success. Keep the old target until a complete reconciliation report is approved.

docs.title: 13. Permissions and audit
docs.text:
• backup.read: view status;
  • backup.run: retry/verify jobs;
  • backup.manage: configure target/encryption;
  • backup.restore: restore data.

Target changes, tests, key export confirmation, durable failures, snapshots, retention deletion and restore are audited. Secrets, OAuth tokens and keys are never included.

docs.title: 14. Operational checklist
docs.text:
• daily: pending/failed counts and last DB snapshot;
  • weekly: target quota/authentication and random archive verification;
  • monthly: database retention and document-vault completeness;
  • quarterly: isolated database + C/M + financial-document restore;
  • after credential/target/version change: connection test and immediate snapshot;
  • incident: preserve logs/audit and do not overwrite the last known-good database.

docs.title: 1. Source layout
docs.text:
English is the default website language. German mirrors every page at the same relative path.

docs/
  en/...
  en/assets/screenshots/...
  de/...
  de/assets/screenshots/...
  assets/screenshot-manifest.json

The root README.md is English; README_DE.md is German. Markdown/MkDocs is the authoritative source. Generated site/ content is not edited manually.

docs.title: 2. Local preview
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv-docs
.\.venv-docs\Scripts\Activate.ps1
pip install -r docs-requirements.txt
python scripts/verify_documentation.py
mkdocs serve

English is available at /, German at /de/. The Material language switcher retains the current relative page.

docs.title: 3. Strict build
docs.text:
mkdocs build --strict --clean

mkdocs-static-i18n uses folder structure with fallback disabled; a missing German counterpart therefore cannot silently display English. mkdocs-redirects preserves the former flat German documentation URLs.

docs.title: 4. Screenshot capture
docs.text:
Run the reproducible capture script from a clean working directory:

.\venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\capture_documentation_screenshots.py

The script uses an isolated temporary configuration/database, disables external services and writes stable semantic PNG names for en-GB and de-DE. Never point it at state/archive.db or production cloud/source credentials.

The screenshot manifest records locale, feature, file and capture profile. Visual review must verify FLEET Mira branding, no clipped controls and no personal/endpoint/credential data.

docs.title: 5. Verification script
docs.text:
scripts/verify_documentation.py checks:

  • exact DE/EN page-path parity;
  • referenced local images exist;
  • screenshot manifest references valid files;
  • no Windows user paths or common secret patterns;
  • no unapproved legacy visible product names;
  • README language links and required top-level pages.

The script is a guardrail, not a substitute for content/visual review.

docs.title: 6. CI publication
docs.text:
.github/workflows/publish-docs.yml installs pinned documentation dependencies, runs verification and mkdocs build --strict --clean, checks out the public technical documentation repository and replaces its generated content.

DOCS_DEPLOY_TOKEN needs write access only to the destination repository. Pull requests should build but should not publish with an untrusted token context.

The destination repository name may retain the old technical DTCONGDownloadTool-docs identifier. The published site title/content remain FLEET Mira.

docs.title: 7. Authoring rules
docs.text:
• Update English and German in the same change.
  • Use identical relative page/image paths per language.
  • Describe implemented behaviour only.
  • Use stable IDs/status/API fields verbatim in code formatting.
  • Use FLEET Mira for visible product text; use DLTNG only in explicit legacy compatibility explanations.
  • Localise Mermaid labels, captions and alt text.
  • Do not commit production DDD files, databases, documents, logs, tokens, server names or user paths.
  • Re-run screenshot capture after a visible UI/navigation change.

docs.title: 8. Release checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] documentation verifier passes;
  • [ ] strict MkDocs build passes;
  • [ ] language switch retains matching page;
  • [ ] old flat URLs redirect;
  • [ ] all screenshots exist in both locales;
  • [ ] README and navigation match current release;
  • [ ] APIs and data contracts match code/version constants;
  • [ ] no secrets or operational data in generated site.

docs.title: Dokumentation von FLEET Mira
docs.text:
FLEET Mira vereint Gerätebetrieb, revisionsnahe Tachodatenarchivierung, Fahrerkarte- und Massenspeicherauswertung, Fuhrparkakten, Führerscheinkontrollen, Fälligkeiten, Finanzbelege und verifizierte Cloud-Sicherung in einer Windows-Anwendung.

Abbildung: Startdashboard von FLEET Mira mit anonymisierten Demonstrationsdaten.

docs.title: Einstieg nach Zielgruppe
docs.text: Zielgruppe Einstieg Inhalt Endanwender Benutzerhandbuch tägliche Abläufe, Listen, Akten, Auswertungen, Belege und Fehlerbehebung Administratoren Administratoren- und Integratorenhandbuch Deployment, Benutzer, Dienste, Sicherheit, Audit, Logs und Wartung API-Integratoren DriverCard REST und FiBu REST Endpunkte, Payloads, Idempotenz und Fehlerbehandlung FiBu-Integratoren FiBu CSV/ZIP Archivaufbau und feldgenaue Datenverträge Betrieb/IT Cloud-Backup und Restore Google Drive/WebDAV, Datenbank-Snapshots und Notfallwiederherstellung

docs.title: Grundprinzipien
docs.text:
• Originale C- und M-Dateien bleiben über Hashwerte und das unveränderte lokale Archiv nachweisbar.
  • Signaturergebnisse werden je signierter Sektion gespeichert; ungültige oder nicht prüfbare Dateien werden quarantänisiert, aber nicht gelöscht.
  • Fachliche Änderungen und automatische Importe werden im globalen Audit protokolliert.
  • Tachographenberechnungen verwenden ausschließlich UTC. Regionseinstellungen verändern nur die Darstellung.
  • Cloud-Backup arbeitet einseitig vom führenden lokalen Archiv zu genau einem aktiven Ziel.
  • Finanzbelege müssen cloudverifiziert sein, bevor eine Fremdrechnung geprüft oder gebucht werden kann.
  • UI-Layout, Sprache und Region werden benutzerbezogen gespeichert.

docs.title: Produkt- und Legacy-Bezeichnungen
docs.text: FLEET Mira ist der sichtbare Produktname. Technische Kennungen wie DLTNGDownloadTool.spec, Datenbanktabellen, API-Pfade, bestehende Konfigurationsschlüssel und das Backuppräfix DLTNG/<Installations-ID>/... bleiben aus Kompatibilitätsgründen unverändert. Sie dürfen in bestehenden Installationen nicht umbenannt werden.

docs.title: Dokumentationsstand
docs.text: Die Dokumentation folgt den aktuellen Menüs und implementierten Diensten. Screenshots verwenden ein reproduzierbares, isoliertes Demoprofil und enthalten keine produktiven DDD-Dateien, Zugangsdaten, URLs oder Personaldaten.

docs.title: 1. Deploymentmodell
docs.text:
FLEET Mira ist eine Windows-Desktopanwendung mit Hintergrundscheduler. Das Anwendungsverzeichnis enthält Python-Module beziehungsweise den PyInstaller-Build, Assets und Übersetzungen. Veränderliche Daten werden über config.json referenziert:

Bestandteil Standard/Zweck SQLite-Datenbank state/archive.db; Schema und Fachmetadaten Tachodatenarchiv komprimierte .ddd.gz-Objekte unterhalb des konfigurierten Archivpfads Logs logs/app.log, REST-Logs und native_crash.log Konfiguration config.json; Pfade, Quellen, Tasks und Dienstvorgaben UI-Profil benutzerbezogenes JSON in SQLite plus lokale QSettings-Fenstergeometrie

Verwenden Sie im Produktivbetrieb absolute Dienstpfade und vergeben Sie Schreibrechte nur an die Anwendungsidentität. Antivirus-Ausnahmen dürfen nur eng begrenzt und nachgewiesen sein; heruntergeladene Originale dürfen nicht pauschal von der Prüfung ausgeschlossen werden.

docs.title: Erstinstallation
docs.text:
1. Build und bereitgestellte leere Deploymentdatenbank ausrollen.
  2. Archiv-, Datenbank- und Logpfade vor dem ersten Import prüfen.
  3. FLEET Mira starten und admin / admin anmelden.
  4. Kennwort auf mindestens 12 Zeichen ändern.
  5. Namensbenutzer anlegen und Rollenbeschränkungen testen.
  6. Quellen/Tasks, SMTP und Backup in dieser Reihenfolge konfigurieren.
  7. Backup-/Restore- und Terminallöschtests durchführen und dokumentieren.

docs.title: 2. Benutzer, Rollen und Berechtigungen
docs.text:
Kennwörter verwenden Argon2id. Fünf Fehlversuche sperren ein Konto für 15 Minuten. Der letzte aktive Administrator kann nicht deaktiviert werden.

Abbildung: Verwaltung von Benutzern, Rollen, Sprachen und Kontostatus.

Rolle Typischer Umfang ADMIN vollständige Administration, Finanzbuchung, Restore und Retention SUPPORT Geräte-/Quellen-/Taskbetrieb, Archivfehlerbehebung, Fuhrpark/Finanzen lesend OPERATOR operative Importe, Fuhrparkpflege, FSK und Finanzabläufe AUDITOR Lesen, Berichte und Exporte ohne Fachänderungen

Die UI deaktiviert unberechtigte Aktionen; Repositories/Dienste prüfen zusätzlich PermissionService.require(...). Ausgeblendete oder deaktivierte Schaltflächen sind nicht die Sicherheitsgrenze.

Lokale Adminwiederherstellung erfolgt über --reset-admin-password, benötigt Windows-Administratorrechte, setzt nur den initialen Adminzugang auf admin, entsperrt das Konto und erzwingt ein neues Kennwort. Das Kennwort selbst wird nie auditiert.

docs.title: 3. Sprachen, Regionen und UTC
docs.text:
Neue Installationen starten Englisch/en-GB. Benutzer können jede registrierte Sprache/Region wählen. Datepicker, Zahlen und Währung folgen der Region; Datenbank/API bleiben ISO. Tachographenzeitwerte, Vergleiche, Tages-/Wochengrenzen und Verstoßberechnungen bleiben aktuell ausschließlich UTC. Die Locale darf keine Zeitzonenumrechnung einführen.

Kataloge liegen unter translations/<sprache>.json. Englisch ist Fallback. Fehlende Produktivschlüssel erzeugen I18N_MISSING und schlagen im Katalogparitätstest fehl.

Abbildung: Administrative Anwendungseinstellungen.

docs.title: 4. SQLite und Migrationen
docs.text:
Migrationen laufen einmal beim Start, bevor Repositories UI-Aufträge bedienen. Eine laufende Datenbankdatei darf nie direkt als Backup kopiert werden.

Betriebsempfehlungen:

  • Datenbank und Archiv auf zuverlässigem lokalen Speicher halten;
  • freien Speicher und fehlgeschlagene Backupjobs überwachen;
  • konsistente Snapshots nur über die integrierte SQLite-Backup-API erzeugen;
  • nach Restore/Wartung PRAGMA integrity_check ausführen;
  • vor manueller Dateiwartung Anwendung und Dienste beenden;
  • Audit-, Signatur- und Finanztabellen niemals direkt ändern.

Die Deploymentdatenbank darf nur Schema/Standardwerte enthalten, keine Personen, Fahrzeuge, DDD-Dateien, Belege, Zugangsdaten oder Auditverläufe.

docs.title: 5. Scheduler, Quellen und Tasks
docs.text:
Der Hintergrundscheduler startet unabhängig von einer UI-Anmeldung als SYSTEM. Unterstützt werden FTP und DLT-API; jeder Netzwerkzugriff benötigt Verbindungs-/Lesetimeout.

flowchart TD
    A[Zeitplan oder Sofort starten] --> B[Passende Remoteobjekte listen]
    B --> C[Kontrolliert lokal herunterladen]
    C --> D[Hash, Klassifizierung und Archiv]
    D --> E[Parser/Signatur oder RFID-CSV]
    E --> F[Verknüpfungen und Fälligkeiten]
    F --> G[Cloudbackup einreihen]
    G --> H{Strenger Backupmodus?}
    H -->|Nein| I[Verarbeitet markieren / optional Terminal löschen]
    H -->|Ja| J[Verifiziertes Cloudobjekt abwarten]
    J --> I

Ein technischer Fehler vor dauerhafter Archivierung darf das Terminalobjekt weder markieren noch löschen. Hashdubletten setzen offene Backup-/Fälligkeitsarbeit fort, ohne ein zweites Archiv anzulegen.

docs.title: 6. Tachodatenarchiv und Signaturvertrauen
docs.text:
Der Dispatcher klassifiziert DRIVER_CARD, VEHICLE_UNIT oder UNKNOWN. C-Dateien verwenden G1 RSA/SHA-1 beziehungsweise G2-ECDSA-Ketten; M-Dateien prüfen signierte Downloadsektionen für G1/G2/G2V2. cryptography ist Pflichtabhängigkeit.

Priorität: INVALID → UNVERIFIABLE → VALID. Der Parserstatus wird auf verifiziert, verifiziert mit Warnungen oder Quarantäne abgebildet. Quarantäne löscht das Original nie, sperrt aber vertrauenswürdige Zuordnung, C-/M-Abgleich, Verstoßsnapshot und „neueste vertrauenswürdige Datei“.

Truststore-Updates erfolgen administrativ, explizit und auditiert. Quelle, Manifest und festgelegte Fingerprints sind zu prüfen. Zertifikate dürfen nicht unbemerkt von beliebigen Servern vertraut werden.

docs.title: 7. Audit und Diagnose
docs.text:
Das globale Audit normalisiert Geräte-, Tachodaten-, Fuhrpark-, Finanz-, Benutzer- und Backupergebnisse. Einträge enthalten UTC-Zeit, Akteur/User-ID, Rolle, Ursprung, Aktion, Objekttyp/-ID, Ergebnis, Korrelations-ID und bereinigte Vorher-/Nachherdaten. Kennwörter, Tokens, API-Keys und Binäroriginale sind verboten.

app.log ist das vollständige Applikationslog mit Scheduler, Downloads, Importen, Jobs, Cachezeiten, Backup und Dienstfehlern. Technische Texte sind immer Englisch. native_crash.log erfasst über faulthandler Prozessfehler nach Initialisierung der Diagnose; Fehler vor Pythonstart können dort nicht erscheinen.

Abbildung: Auditnachweise im Zusammenspiel mit der technischen Diagnose.

Der UI-Heartbeat schreibt UI_STALL, wenn die Ereignisschleife länger als eine Sekunde blockiert. Nach Erholung folgt die Dauer. Job-, Request- und Korrelations-IDs verbinden UI-, Dienst- und Auditvorgänge.

docs.title: 8. UI-Jobbetrieb
docs.text:
Teure Abfragen/Berechnungen verwenden workerlokale SQLite-Verbindungen; Qt-Widgets werden nur im GUI-Thread verändert.
